title: Specialized Information and Referral * Blindness - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Specialized Information and Referral * Blindness 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Assistive Technology Issues Blindness Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Deafness Disabilities Issues Down Syndrome Mental Health Issues Older Adult/Aging Issues Immigration Issues Abused Lesbians/Gay Individuals Abused Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AMERICAN COUNCIL OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral for the non-medical aspects of blindness, support to consumer advocates, guide dog information, and some legal information on matters relating to blindness. Publishes the ACB Braille Forum, which is available in braille, large print, digital cartridge, as well as via podcasts, email, or downloaded. 225 Reinekers Lane Suite 660 Alexandria, VA 22314 Eligibility: People who are blind or visually impaired Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-2pm Email (800) 424-8666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals achieve independence and equality by providing information and referral, employment information, literature, scholarship programs, and a blindness-related adaptive equipment store. The Free White Cane Program provides rigid fiberglass canes. 200 E Wells Street Baltimore, MD 21230 Eligibility: -Primarily blind and visually impaired individuals, but sighted persons may also join -Free White Cane Program: Must be blind or visually impaired, must be requesting the cane for personal use, and it must have been more than 6 months since a previous white cane request Hours: Varies by chapter National office: Monday-Friday 5am-2pm Email (541) 653-9153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
